_comment_groups: Actual final draw held 5 Dec 2025 in Washington, DC. Team names use the canonical spelling produced by core.config.normalize_team so they join cleanly to Elo history and squads.
_comment_host_groups: Each host plays all three group games in its own country, so any group match involving the host (in its group) is non-neutral with the host as the home side.
_comment_r32_template: Round-of-32 pairing template for the new 48-team format. Tokens: W_x = winner of group x, R_x = runner-up of group x, T_n = the n-th best of the eight qualifying third-placed teams (T_1 = best). The template avoids winner-vs-runner-up rematches from the same group; third-placed teams are slotted at simulation time so they never meet their own group's winner. This is a faithful, documented bracket for the 24 group qualifiers + 8 best thirds; FIFA's exact third-place-combination slotting table is approximated (see README / Under-the-Hood).
_comment_known_results: Optional: lock in already-played group matches so the simulator runs forward from the live state. Each entry: {"home": str, "away": str, "home_score": int, "away_score": int}. Team names are normalized on load. Empty by default (no unverified scores committed). Populate from the live openfootball feed via `python scripts/update_results.py --write`, or refresh on demand in the app. `known_results_as_of` is the ISO-8601 fetch timestamp (null until populated). Only group-stage results are locked; knockout matches are re-simulated.
